summary: Linked Kirby's copied Donkey Kong neutral special source by marking `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` as matching, assigning its `.sdata2` range in splits, converting three sdata2 symbols from temporary global `ftKb_Init_*` names to local compiler labels, and adding a small helper to force the expected `S32_TO_F32` double constant ordering. The source also removed unnecessary function-pointer casts on callback assignments once the referenced callback prototypes/types were compatible.
what_changed: `config/GALE01/splits.txt` added the `.sdata2` slice for `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` at `start:0x804D9488 end:0x804D9498` alongside its existing `.text` range `0x800FF8EC..0x80100E0C`.

`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` renamed `.sdata2` entries at `0x804D9488`, `0x804D9490`, and `0x804D9494` from global `ftKb_Init_804D9488`, `ftKb_Init_804D9490`, and `ftKb_Init_804D9494` symbols to local labels `@222`, `@233`, and `@234`.

`ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` added a static `double sdata2_order0(void)` helper returning `S32_TO_F32`, with a todo noting it appears to model an inline within `ftKb_DkSpecialN_Anim` shared by `ftKb_DkSpecialAirN_Anim`.
`ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` simplified `fp->death2_cb` and `fp->take_dmg_cb` assignments in `ftKb_DkSpecialNStart_Coll_inline` by removing explicit casts to `void (*)(HSD_GObj*)`.
smart_moves: Handled the link as both a source match and data-split problem: the PR did not only flip `NonMatching` to `Matching`, it also claimed the exact `.sdata2` range needed by the object.
Used a tiny static helper returning `S32_TO_F32` to influence local constant emission/order without adding larger behavioral changes.
Converted placeholder-looking global sdata2 names into local labels once the constants belonged to the linked object, reducing misleading `ftKb_Init_*` symbol leakage.
Removed redundant callback casts instead of preserving cast noise, suggesting the source could match while keeping clearer typed callback assignments.
decomp_lessons: When linking a previously nonmatching fighter file, verify both `.text` and any local constant sections such as `.sdata2`; missing data ranges can block clean linking even when codegen is close.
Compiler-generated local float/double constants may need local `@NNN` symbols rather than artificial global labels once the owning object is linked.
Small helper functions can be used as ordering anchors for constants when matching Metrowerks emission order, but should be documented when their exact source-level intent is uncertain.
Unnecessary function-pointer casts can sometimes be removed after header/prototype cleanup; matching does not always require preserving decompiler-era cast artifacts.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied-special files use lowercase filenames such as `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` under `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/`.
Functions in this file retain `ftKb_` prefixes, e.g. `ftKb_SpecialNDk_800FF8EC`, `ftKb_SpecialNDk_800FFA10`, and `ftKb_DkSpecialNStart_Coll_inline`.
Temporary or unknown local sdata2 constants are represented with local labels such as `@222`, `@233`, and `@234` rather than named global symbols when evidence supports local scope.
The helper name `sdata2_order0` is explicitly technical and order-oriented, not a claimed retail symbol; the added todo acknowledges uncertainty.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added `.sdata2 start:0x804D9488 end:0x804D9498` to the object's split to capture its local double/float constants.
Returned `S32_TO_F32` from a static `double` helper to force the expected double constant at `0x804D9488` and preserve sdata2 ordering.
Updated symbol scope from global to local for the affected `.sdata2` constants, matching how compiler-local constants are represented.
Removed explicit casts on `ftKb_Init_800EE74C` and `ftKb_Init_800EE7B8` callback assignments, likely relying on correct function pointer type compatibility for cleaner codegen/source.
searchable_terms: Kirby copied Donkey Kong neutral special
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove or rename `sdata2_order0` after identifying the real shared inline between `ftKb_DkSpecialN_Anim` and `ftKb_DkSpecialAirN_Anim`.
Search for other Kirby copied-special files that required adding `.sdata2` splits before switching from `NonMatching` to `Matching`.
Look for PRs converting `ftKb_Init_804D*` global sdata2 placeholder symbols into local `@NNN` labels.
Check whether callback prototypes for `death2_cb` and `take_dmg_cb` were standardized before this PR, enabling cast removal.
